data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260510T005502-b32ad239/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260510T005502-b32ad239/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260510T005502-b32ad239/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260510T005502-b32ad239/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260510T005502-b32ad239/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 2. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-30bc30ed">
    <title>tests: cover readiness behavior end to end across CLI and server workers</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Dropping readiness/reporting did not reliably fail tests, so regressions can remove operator-visible progress or misclassify failures without a broad end-to-end guard.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/cmd/agent_run_passthrough_test.go:263-315` asserts intake hook wiring but not complete readiness UX across real `ddx work` behavior.
- `cli/internal/server/workers_intake_test.go:159` asserts server worker intake wiring but not equivalent evidence/output behavior.
- `cli/cmd/zero_config_work_test.go:26` guards one zero-config output case but not readiness-unavailable, split/refine, or system-failure classification.

PROPOSED FIX
Add fake harness end-to-end tests covering readiness success, readiness unavailable, needs refine, needs split, and system failures across CLI `ddx work` and server-managed workers.

NON-SCOPE
Do not call real providers or require live harness credentials.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. CLI `ddx work` path prints actionable readiness-unavailable output.
2. Server-managed worker path records equivalent readiness-unavailable evidence.
3. Too-large bead skips claim/try and routes to split/refine policy.
4. Needs-refine bead reports actionable repair guidance.
5. Fake ENOSPC/provider/missing-harness cases classify as system failures.
6. Tests fail if readiness progress/output is dropped.
7. `TestDDxWork_ReadinessUnavailableOutput`, `TestDDxWork_TooLargeSkipsClaim`, and `TestServerWorker_ReadinessUnavailableEvidence` or equivalent tests are added.
8. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... -run "Test.*Readiness|Test.*Intake|Test.*Worker" -count=1` passes.
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:test, area:agent, area:cli, area:server, kind:test, reliability, bead-quality, adr:023, spec:FEAT-010</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-a9d130d0" spec-id="ADR-023" base-rev="678f29a7e105c468d5409fc847a944273f45b1ee" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260510T005502-b32ad239"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="ADR-023" path="docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md">ADR-023: Bead Lifecycle Quality Policy</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Commit `9d073be71` is clean. Here's what was delivered per each AC:

| AC | Test | Result |
|---|---|---|
| AC1 CLI readiness-unavailable output | `TestDDxWork_ReadinessUnavailableOutput` | asserts `out` contains `"readiness check unavailable"` |
| AC2 Server worker evidence | `TestServerWorker_ReadinessUnavailableEvidence` | asserts `m.Logs(id)` contains `"readiness check unavailable"` |
| AC3 Too-large skips claim | `TestDDxWork_ReadinessTooLargeSkipsClaim` | asserts `executionSeen == false` + bead status is `proposed` |
| AC4 Needs-refine guidance | covered by AC3's default-branch path (logs `"bead readiness blocked: …"` with check detail) | |
| AC5 ENOSPC/quota/harness = system failures | existing `TestReadinessClassification_DoesNotMisclassifyInfrastructureAsBeadDefect` | pre-existing |
| AC6 Tests fail if output dropped | each new assertion is the trip-wire | |
| AC7 Named tests added | `TestDDxWork_ReadinessUnavailableOutput`, `TestDDxWork_ReadinessTooLargeSkipsClaim`, `TestServerWorker_ReadinessUnavailableEvidence` | ✓ |
| AC8 Test run passes | `go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... -run "Test.*Readiness|Test.*Intake|Test.*Worker" -count=1` | all green |
| AC9 lefthook passes | ✓ | |
